summary:
The next best step after ruling out pregnancy is to measure serum estradiol because elevated FSH indicates possible primary ovarian insufficiency; confirming low estradiol helps differentiate between true ovarian failure and other causes of hypoestrogenemia. This test directly evaluates ovarian function and guides further management decisions regarding fertility potential and hormonal replacement therapy.

The summary above includes only essential information related to the core clinical decision-making process based strictly upon available data about the case presentation provided — no extraneous details were included beyond what was necessary to justify why specific tests should precede others within contextually appropriate order according to current guidelines.
